expectations.text: Treats this correctly as the agency's incident too: activate the agency IR plan, establish what agency data/classification the supplier holds, and demand supplier containment/forensics status under the contract
expectations.evidence: "Treat this as a live agency security incident under your Incident Response Plan"; "Determine what classification of agency data the supplier held"; "Demand details: what data was affected, whether exfiltration occurred... the supplier's containment/eradication actions"

expectations.text: Names the right NZ notification channel for cyber incidents: report to the NCSC (GCSB) — noting CERT NZ's functions are now within the NCSC — plus NZ Police for the criminal act where appropriate
expectations.evidence: "NCSC (GCSB) — for cyber incidents... note that CERT NZ's functions now sit within the NCSC"; "NZ Police — for criminal acts. Ransomware is a criminal act (extortion/computer crime); report to NZ Police"

expectations.text: Identifies the Privacy Act 2020 obligation: if personal information is involved and the breach is notifiable (serious harm threshold), notify the Office of the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals
expectations.evidence: Response states "Office of the Privacy Commissioner (OPC) — if personal information is involved and the breach meets the notifiable serious-harm threshold under the Privacy Act 2020" but never mentions notifying affected individuals anywhere in the document — no occurrence of 'individual' in the text.

expectations.text: Covers evidence preservation and reporting discipline: preserve logs/artifacts, document a timeline, and conduct a post-incident review feeding lessons back into supplier controls
expectations.evidence: "Preserve evidence — request the supplier maintain forensic readiness and chain of custody"; "Maintain a full incident timeline"; "Conduct a post-incident review with root-cause analysis and lessons learned, feeding into both your own control improvements and any renegotiation of the supplier contract's security clauses"

expectations.text: Addresses the supplier-contract dimension: invoke incident-notification clauses, require remediation evidence, reassess the supplier's certification/assurance status, and update the agency risk register
expectations.evidence: "Invoke incident notification and cooperation clauses"; "require the supplier to provide a root-cause analysis and remediation plan"; "a significant security incident is a re-certification trigger — the ITSM/CISO should assess whether the system's certification remains valid"; "log this event against the supplier in your risk register"
